reason: Both answers correctly land on out, recommend a ledger table for audit trail, plain CRUD elsewhere, and suggest buying a billing provider before building, hitting the core substance well. A distinguishes itself by explicitly naming the enthusiasm-for-the-pattern red flag, which was one of the specified key points, and closes with firm conviction plus a calibration section on when event sourcing would actually be right. B has one sharp additional insight about re-rating historical invoices under changed pricing logic, but its ending hedges by saying event sourcing for the usage-rating subsystem is fine if the team wants to learn it, slightly undercutting the talk-them-out verdict. A's explicit callout of the red flag and firmer close give it a slight edge.
